mechanism_narrative: Total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) exhibits a persistent upward trend that reflects underlying economic expansion; when PAYEMS is at or above its 12-month moving average, it signals ongoing growth that supports positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return correlation with PAYEMS deviation from trend is not significantly positive (Rank IC < 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window) or if the signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of cases when PAYEMS is above its moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above 1,200 thousand units annually signals robust construction activity and consumer confidence, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the economic multiplier effects materialize.
failure_condition: If the average forward 60-day return after HOUST exceeds 1200 is not statistically greater than the unconditional average return (t-test p > 0.10) or Rank IC over trailing 3-year window is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains near zero, the real interest rate becomes deeply negative, distorting capital allocation and ultimately leading to a corrective sell-off in equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (CPIAUCSL rising and FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%) does not produce negative average forward returns, or if Rank IC is positive over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) month-over-month growth predicts positive S&P 500 forward 5-day returns only during economic expansions when the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below 6%, as output gains in such regimes reflect sustainable demand rather than inventory build.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE below 6% defines expansion for this mechanism
failure_condition: If during expansion periods (UNRATE < 6%) the Rank IC of INDPRO growth on forward 5-day returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or if the signal is significant only out-of-expansion.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals labor market deterioration that immediately impacts consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp in 3 months, the average forward 5-day return is not negative or if the signal is not statistically significant at p<0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates synchronized demand-supply expansion that reinforces corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (both series above their 3-month moving average) does not produce average forward 60-day returns significantly above the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC below 0.02 over 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is inversely related to equity valuations through the discount rate mechanism; persistently low or declining rates provide a tailwind for S&P 500 returns, so higher rates signal headwinds and predict negative forward returns.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20-day returns is not negative, or if the Rank IC of the 'low rate' signal (FEDFUNDS below median) does not produce positive returns at IC>0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% annualized rate signals above-trend economic expansion that may lead to overheating and monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the forward 60-day average return following quarters with GDP growth > 3% is not negative or if the signal's Rank IC does not remain below 0 over a 5-year walk-forward.
